intro: The puppets have a problem! Watch what happens, then help them find a kind way.
cast-prompt: Pick two puppets for the show!
what-now: What could they do? Watch each idea!
your-turn: Now you choose! Tap the idea you like best.
nudge-retry: Hmm, that made a sad face. Let's try a kinder way!
truck-setup: One puppet is playing with the toy truck. Here comes their friend!
truck-problem: Uh oh! Both friends want the same truck.
truck-ask-grab: Should the friend grab the truck away?
truck-after-grab: Oh no. Grabbing made their friend feel sad. Let's pick a kinder idea!
truck-ask-ask: Should the friend ask for a turn, please?
truck-after-ask: Asking with kind words worked! Both friends feel happy.
truck-ask-share: Or could they roll the truck together?
truck-after-share: Sharing means everyone gets to play. Wonderful!
red-crayon-setup: Both friends are drawing. But there is only one red crayon!
red-crayon-problem: Uh oh! Both friends want the same red crayon.
red-crayon-ask-grab: Should the friend grab the red crayon away?
red-crayon-after-grab: Oh no. Grabbing made their friend feel sad. Let's pick a kinder idea!
red-crayon-ask-turns: Should they take turns with the red crayon?
red-crayon-after-turns: Taking turns means everyone gets the red crayon. Wonderful!
red-crayon-ask-offer: Or could one friend offer the red crayon first?
red-crayon-after-offer: Offering first is so kind. Both friends feel happy!
storybook-setup: One puppet has the only storybook. Their friend wants to look too!
storybook-problem: Uh oh! Only one storybook, and both friends want to see.
storybook-ask-hide: Should the puppet hide the storybook away?
storybook-after-hide: Oh no. Hiding it made their friend feel sad. Let's try a kinder idea!
storybook-ask-together: Could they look at the storybook together, side by side?
storybook-after-together: Looking together, both friends can see the story. Wonderful!
storybook-ask-turns: Or could they take turns holding the storybook?
storybook-after-turns: Taking turns means everyone gets to hold the book. Great sharing!
big-ball-setup: One puppet is bouncing the big ball. Here comes their friend!
big-ball-problem: Uh oh! One friend has the ball, and both want to play.
big-ball-ask-alone: Should the puppet turn away and play alone?
big-ball-after-alone: Oh no. Playing alone made their friend feel sad. Let's pick a kinder idea!
big-ball-ask-roll: Could they roll the big ball back and forth together?
big-ball-after-roll: Rolling it together, both friends get to play. Wonderful!
big-ball-ask-turn: Or could the friend have a turn with the ball?
big-ball-after-turn: Sharing a turn made both friends happy. Great kindness!
slide-setup: Two friends run to the slide. They both want to go first!
slide-problem: Uh oh! Both friends want to be first.
slide-ask-push: Should one friend push past to go first?
slide-after-push: Oh no. Pushing wasn't kind. Let's try a friendlier idea!
slide-ask-youfirst: Should one friend let their friend go first?
slide-after-youfirst: Taking turns going first made both friends happy!
slide-ask-count: Or could they take turns, one then the other?
slide-after-count: Taking turns means everyone gets a turn. Well done!
swing-setup: One friend has been swinging for a long, long time.
swing-problem: Their friend is still waiting for a turn to swing.
swing-ask-ignore: Should the friend keep swinging and not share?
swing-after-ignore: Waiting so long felt sad. Let's find a kinder idea!
swing-ask-offer: Should the friend hop off and offer a turn?
swing-after-offer: Hopping off to share made both friends happy!
swing-ask-turns: Or could they take turns on the swing?
swing-after-turns: Taking turns means everyone gets to swing. Wonderful!
drum-setup: One puppet is banging the toy drum. Here comes their friend!
drum-problem: Uh oh! Both friends want to play the drum.
drum-ask-grab: Should the friend grab the drumsticks away?
drum-after-grab: Oh no. Grabbing made their friend feel sad. Let's pick a kinder idea!
drum-ask-turns: Should the friends take turns drumming?
drum-after-turns: Taking turns means everyone gets to drum. Well done!
drum-ask-together: Or could they play together, one taps and one claps?
drum-after-together: Playing together, everyone makes music. Wonderful!
telescope-setup: One puppet is looking through the telescope. Here comes their friend!
telescope-problem: Oh no. Their friend wants a turn to look too.
telescope-ask-keep: Should the friend keep looking and say it's mine?
telescope-after-keep: Keeping it all felt unfair. Let's try a kinder idea!
telescope-ask-yourturn: Should the friend say your turn now?
telescope-after-yourturn: Giving a turn made both friends happy!
telescope-ask-turns: Or could they take turns looking and telling what they see?
telescope-after-turns: Taking turns, both friends get to look and share. Wonderful!
high-shelf-setup: One puppet wants the ball on top of the tall shelf.
high-shelf-problem: The ball is too high. One puppet cannot reach it alone.
high-shelf-ask-jump: Should one puppet keep jumping all alone?
high-shelf-after-jump: Trying alone again and again is so tiring. Let's pick a kinder idea!
high-shelf-ask-help: Should one puppet ask their friend to help?
high-shelf-after-help: Asking for help worked! Now both friends can play.
high-shelf-ask-giveup: Should one puppet give up and feel sad?
high-shelf-after-giveup: Giving up feels sad. Let's try asking a friend instead!
spilled-blocks-setup: One puppet is playing with a big pile of blocks.
spilled-blocks-problem: Oh no! The blocks spilled everywhere. What a big mess.
spilled-blocks-ask-run: Should one puppet run away from the big mess?
spilled-blocks-after-run: Running away leaves the mess and feels sad. Let's pick a kinder idea!
spilled-blocks-ask-help: Should one puppet ask their friend to help clean up?
spilled-blocks-after-help: Asking for help made cleaning quick and happy!
spilled-blocks-ask-together: Or could a friend offer to help clean together?
spilled-blocks-after-together: Cleaning up together is fast and fun. Wonderful teamwork!
stuck-wagon-setup: One puppet wants to pull the little red wagon.
stuck-wagon-problem: The wagon is stuck! One puppet cannot pull it alone.
stuck-wagon-ask-yank: Should one puppet yank hard and stomp all alone?
stuck-wagon-after-yank: Yanking and stomping is tiring and sad. Let's pick a kinder idea!
stuck-wagon-ask-help: Should one puppet ask their friend for help?
stuck-wagon-after-help: Asking for help got the wagon moving. Hooray!
stuck-wagon-ask-together: Or could both friends push and pull together?
stuck-wagon-after-together: Pushing and pulling together, the wagon rolls! Great teamwork!
tangled-kite-setup: One puppet has a kite, but the string is all tangled.
tangled-kite-problem: What a big tangle! One puppet cannot fix it alone.
tangled-kite-ask-pull: Should one puppet pull the string harder all alone?
tangled-kite-after-pull: Pulling hard made the tangle worse. Let's pick a kinder idea!
tangled-kite-ask-help: Should one puppet ask their friend to help untangle it?
tangled-kite-after-help: Asking for help fixed the tangle. Now the kite can fly!
tangled-kite-ask-together: Or could both friends work on it together, patiently?
tangled-kite-after-together: Working together patiently, the tangle is gone! Wonderful!
